title: Peruvian Grilled Pork Tenderloin
description: A flavorful and tender Peruvian-style grilled pork tenderloin recipe. This dish is perfect for a summer evening dinner.

ingredients.name: Monsoon chili powder
ingredients.notes: Available at local spice stores or online

ingredients.name: Tacu
ingredients.notes: Peruvian seasoning mix available at local Latin American markets

instructions: In a small bowl, mix together Monsoon chili powder, Tacu, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork tenderloin, making sure to coat it evenly.
Drizzle olive oil over the pork tenderloin and rub it in to prevent sticking.
Grill the pork tenderloin for 5-7 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F.
Let the pork tenderloin rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly.
Serve with a squeeze of fresh lime juice and your choice of sides.
